target.name: QuantumScape
target.founder: Jagdeep Singh, Tim Holme, Fritz Prinz

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $1.0B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $800M+

data.narrative:
QuantumScape is a publicly traded frontier-energy company in the Climate & Energy sector, currently valued above $5B after raising $2B+ in total capital. The valuation assessment draws from a peer set of 8 same-sector, similar-valuation-tier companies, predominantly private (8 comps) with one public reference point (LanzaTech, ticker LNZA). Key private peers include TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, and Commonwealth Fusion Systems.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given available data. The median valuation across the 8-company peer set is $4.2B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $6.0B. Sample peers occupy a tight cluster: TAE Technologies and Redwood Materials at $6.0B, Helion at $5.4B, Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B, and Form Energy at $3.5B. This method directly reflects market pricing of comparable companies at analogous development stages and capital intensity.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $3.6B to $4.9B, centered on $4.2B. QuantumScape's stated $5B+ current valuation sits modestly above the peer median, suggesting a modest premium relative to the comparable set. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation relative to peers (solid-state battery maturity and commercialization timeline) and capital requirements to reach production scale. Limited public market data constrains precision; the single public reference point provides only indirect calibration.
